Reading, made calm again

The news, laid out like the page it was printed to be.

No feed. No noise. Just the publication's own front page — curated by its editors, quiet on your eyes, and yours to keep even offline.

Choose how to read See the idea

Chapter one

Somewhere along the way, reading the news got loud.

Endless feeds, pop-ups, the next thing and the next. E-paper steps back to something steadier: a real newspaper layout you can hold in one hand. The same page that printed, the same order the editors intended — only lighter, and always with you.

Chapter two · The new chapter

Four things print
could never do.

01 — Read

The printed page, as it is

Open any story and it stays faithful to the page it ran on — clean type, the editor’s framing, nothing rearranged.

02 — Layout

A digital layout, built for reading

The full front page, section tabs and all — the printed grid, re-rendered to sit comfortably in your hand.

03 — Share

Stories worth sharing

Found something worth talking about? Pass the page along in a tap — the same layout lands on the other side.

04 — Zoom

Lean in and magnify

Pinch into any column and the page holds crisp — read the fine print as if it were under glass.

The front page, in your hands

Your chosen title, every morning — with 14 days of past issues always within reach.
